differential_diagnoses: Occult infection (e.g., Cat scratch disease, Q fever, or brucellosis)
Systemic lupus erythematosus or other connective tissue disease
next_steps: Obtain thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, total T3)
Perform diagnostic thyroid ultrasonography with Doppler flow assessment
Measure erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR) and C-reactive protein (CRP) again
Obtain antithyroglobulin and anti-thyroid peroxidase antibodies
Consider diagnostic aspiration or biopsy if thyroid nodule persists or appears suspicious on imaging
Repeat infectious disease serologies if symptoms persist (e.g., Bartonella, Q fever, brucellosis)

differential_diagnoses: Thyrotoxicosis (e.g., Graves' disease or toxic nodular goiter)
next_steps: Obtain thyroid function panel (TSH, free T4, total T3)
Perform thyroid ultrasound with Doppler flow assessment
Measure thyroid autoantibodies (TSI, TPO, and antithyroglobulin)
Measure serum ferritin to assess for inflammatory or rheumatologic triggers
Measure antinuclear antibody (ANA) to screen for connective tissue disease
Consider a radioactive iodine uptake and scan (RAIU) to differentiate between destructive thyroiditis and hyperthyroidism
Evaluate for clinical features of thyroid storm and initiate symptomatic management of tachycardia with beta-blockers if indicated
